creator: imb@protected-networks.net
text:
Dell Latitude 5540 (with i7-1355U) also uses SOF on Ubuntu; no HDMI audio on FreeBSD ..

pciconf -l -v yields:

hdac0@pci0:0:31:3:      class=0x040380 rev=0x01 hdr=0x00 vendor=0x8086 device=0x51ca subvendor=0x1028 subdevice=0x0c05
    vendor     = 'Intel Corporation'
    device     = 'Raptor Lake-P/U/H cAVS'
    class      = multimedia
    subclass   = HDA

dmesg reports (after manually patching headphone output):

hdac0: <Intel Raptor Lake-P HDA Controller> mem 0x6055290000-0x6055293fff,0x6055000000-0x60550fffff at device 31.3 on pci0
hdacc0: <Realtek ALC236 HDA CODEC> at cad 0 on hdac0
hdaa0: <Realtek ALC236 Audio Function Group> at nid 1 on hdacc0
pcm0: <Realtek ALC236 (Analog 2.0+HP/2.0)> at nid 20,33 and 18 on hdaa0

/dev/sndstat shows:

FreeBSD Audio Driver
Installed devices:
pcm0: <Realtek ALC236 (Analog 2.0+HP/2.0)> on hdaa0 (1p:1v/1r:1v) default


Ubuntu syslog on boot ..

2025-01-11T14:37:17.872750+00:00 ubuntu kernel: snd_hda_intel 0000:00:1f.3: enabling device (0000 -> 0002)

2025-01-11T14:37:17.872803+00:00 ubuntu kernel: snd_hda_codec_realtek hdaudioC0D0: autoconfig for ALC3204: line_outs=1 (0x14/0x0/0x0/0x0/0x0) type:speaker
2025-01-11T14:37:17.872805+00:00 ubuntu kernel: snd_hda_codec_realtek hdaudioC0D0:    speaker_outs=0 (0x0/0x0/0x0/0x0/0x0)
2025-01-11T14:37:17.872822+00:00 ubuntu kernel: snd_hda_codec_realtek hdaudioC0D0:    hp_outs=1 (0x21/0x0/0x0/0x0/0x0)
2025-01-11T14:37:17.872823+00:00 ubuntu kernel: snd_hda_codec_realtek hdaudioC0D0:    mono: mono_out=0x0
